title: Robots and AI in Our Lives
passage:
Robots and AI in Our Lives

Robots are machines. They can move and do different tasks. Today, we have many robots around us. Some robots work in big factories. They make cars, phones, and computers. These robots work very fast and they do not get tired. They can work all day and all night.

There are also robots in our homes. For example, many people have robot vacuum cleaners. These small, round robots clean the floor. They move around the rooms and clean up the dust. They make life easy for busy people. There are also toy robots. Children can play with them and teach them to walk or sing.

Now, we also have Artificial Intelligence, or AI. AI is like the "brain" of a computer or a robot. AI helps machines think, learn, and make decisions. For example, some smartphones have AI. You can speak to your phone, and the AI answers your questions. The AI learns your voice and knows your favorite music. Some cars also use AI to drive without a human driver.

In some schools, robots help teachers. They play games with students and help them learn languages. In hospitals, robots carry food and medicine to sick people. They can also clean hospital rooms. This helps doctors and nurses save time so they can help more patients.

In the future, we will see more robots and AI. Some people think robots will do all the difficult and dangerous work. Other people worry that robots will take away our jobs. But today, robots and AI are very useful helpers in our daily lives.

We must learn how to use robots and AI. Children in schools are learning how to build simple robots. This is a very fun and useful skill. In the future, many jobs will need people who know how to work with AI.

questions.text: Where do some robots work to make cars?
questions.explanation: The passage states: 'Some robots work in big factories. They make cars, phones, and computers.'

questions.explanation: The passage states: 'These small, round robots clean the floor.'

questions.explanation: The passage states: 'AI is like the "brain" of a computer or a robot.'

questions.instruction: Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.
questions.text: How do robots help doctors and nurses in hospitals?
questions.explanation: The passage states: 'In hospitals, robots carry food and medicine to sick people.'

questions.instruction: Do the following statements agree with the information given in the reading passage? Write TRUE, FALSE or NOT GIVEN.
questions.text: Factory robots get tired after working all night.
questions.explanation: The passage states: 'These robots work very fast and they do not get tired. They can work all day and all night.'

questions.instruction: Do the following statements agree with the information given in the reading passage? Write TRUE, FALSE or NOT GIVEN.
questions.text: Children can teach toy robots to walk or sing.
questions.explanation: The passage states: 'Children can play with them and teach them to walk or sing.'

questions.instruction: Do the following statements agree with the information given in the reading passage? Write TRUE, FALSE or NOT GIVEN.
questions.text: All cars now use AI to drive without human drivers.
questions.explanation: The passage states: 'Some cars also use AI to drive without a human driver,' not 'all' cars.

questions.instruction: Do the following statements agree with the information given in the reading passage? Write TRUE, FALSE or NOT GIVEN.
questions.text: Some robots help teachers in schools.
questions.explanation: The passage states: 'In some schools, robots help teachers.'

questions.instruction: Choose NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS from the passage for each answer.
questions.text: Robot vacuum cleaners help to clean up the ______ from the floor.
questions.explanation: The passage states: 'They move around the rooms and clean up the dust.'

questions.instruction: Choose NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS from the passage for each answer.
questions.text: AI can help machines think, learn, and make ______.
questions.explanation: The passage states: 'AI helps machines think, learn, and make decisions.'

questions.instruction: Choose NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS from the passage for each answer.
questions.text: In hospitals, robots can clean hospital ______.
questions.explanation: The passage states: 'They can also clean hospital rooms.'

questions.instruction: Choose NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS from the passage for each answer.
questions.text: Children in schools are learning how to build simple ______.
questions.explanation: The passage states: 'Children in schools are learning how to build simple robots.'

vocabulary.meaning: jobs or pieces of work that need to be done
buildings where people make things using machines
machines that clean floors by sucking up dirt and dust
choices that you make after thinking
people who are receiving medical treatment in a hospital

tactics:
Hướng dẫn làm bài đạt điểm cao (A1-A2)

1. Tìm từ khóa trực tiếp: Ở trình độ A1-A2, các từ khóa trong câu hỏi sẽ xuất hiện giống hệt hoặc gần như giống hệt trong bài đọc. Hãy dùng kỹ thuật Scanning để tìm nhanh các từ như 'factories', 'vacuum cleaners', 'decisions'.

2. Lưu ý bẫy từ chỉ số lượng: Đối với câu hỏi True/False/Not Given, hãy cẩn thận với các từ chỉ tần suất/số lượng như 'all' (tất cả) so với 'some' (một vài) trong bài đọc.

3. Giới hạn từ: Đọc kỹ yêu cầu số lượng từ (NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS). Từ điền vào chỗ trống phải được lấy chính xác từ bài đọc, không tự ý thay đổi dạng từ (ví dụ: giữ nguyên số nhiều/số ít).
